message:
fix(close): guard against silently orphaning dot-notation children on parent close

Block `br close <parent>` when the parent has open dot-notation
children (e.g. `epic.1`, `epic.2`) that aren't tracked by the formal
parent-child dependency graph. Users can still force the close with
`--force`, matching the existing skip-with-reason pattern for other
guard checks.

Background
- The existing epic-close guard reads `storage.get_epic_counts(id)`,
  which counts open children via the `dependencies` table. That's
  the happy path: `br create --parent epic` writes a
  `parent-child` dep row at creation time.
- Three workflows bypass that dep-row write and leave behind
  dot-notation children with no matching dep:
    * Legacy-bd → beads_rust JSONL migrations.
    * Bulk `import_from_jsonl` runs of externally generated data.
    * Hand-edited issues.jsonl (bd-2xyz.1, bd-2xyz.2, …).
- Without the supplementary check, `br close epic` would succeed
  cleanly and silently leave every dot-child open, with no dep row
  to make the orphan visible. Found in the wild during a legacy-bd
  migration audit (beads_rust-g2w3 follow-up).

src/storage/sqlite.rs
- New public `get_open_dot_notation_children(parent_id) ->
  Result<Vec<String>>` on SqliteStorage.
- Queries: `SELECT id FROM issues WHERE status IN ('open',
  'in_progress') AND (is_template = 0 OR is_template IS NULL)
  AND id LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\' AND id NOT LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\'` where
  the first pattern is `<parent>.%` (direct children) and the
  second is `<parent>.%.%` (grandchildren, to stay direct-only).
- LIKE metachars in the parent id are escaped via
  `escape_like_pattern` so arbitrary ids are safe to pass.
- New unit-test block (appended to mod tests) covers: no children,
  only closed children, open direct child surfaced, grandchildren
  excluded, escape metachar safety.

src/cli/commands/close.rs
- In `execute_route`, after the existing `epic_counts` guard but
  before calling `storage.close_issue(...)`, invoke
  `get_open_dot_notation_children(id)` and push a `SkippedIssue`
  with a user-facing reason when the list is non-empty.
- Reason format: "<label> has N open dot-notation child issue(s):
  id1, id2, ... (+K more) (use --force to close anyway)" — label is
  "epic" when the parent has IssueType::Epic, otherwise the generic
  "parent issue" so the same guard works for non-epic parents.
- Skipped entry is pushed into both `ordered_outcomes` (as
  CloseOutcome::Skipped) and `skipped_issues` so the existing
  JSON/text renderers surface it identically to other skip reasons.
- `--force` short-circuits the guard in the same place as the
  existing skips, preserving the "I know what I'm doing" escape hatch.
